body.title: [sprint] Consequence check: role-ladder dispatch routing
body.description:
**What changed:** Ticket routing moved from worker= direct assignment to role→worker map; audit gained role-fit check; Granny escalation dedup persisted across restart.

**Predicted unintended effects:**
- Tickets with no role field and ambiguous worker field silently mis-route (backward-compat fallback unclear)
- role_map in granny.yaml diverges from actual available workers (config drift)
- DS receives creator-tier tickets (analyst model) but toolloop behavior is identical — model upgrade has no effect if system prompt doesn't change
- Audit role-fit warning treated as a block by future automation that checks audit.passed

**Signals to watch:** HOLD count with 'max iter' signature (should drop); audit warnings appearing in channel for correctly-scoped tickets (false positive rate); DS creator-tier tickets completing vs still hitting max-iter

**Gate condition:** Check by 2026-06-17 or when 5+ role-based tickets have been dispatched through the new routing.

**Worst-case token spend:**
  - Per-failure cost: misconfigured role_map routes all tickets to CC (master) → each ticket costs a full CC sprint (~$0.10–0.50). With 10 queued tickets that's $1–5 worst case.
  - Hard cap: Granny semaphore + cc_concurrency_mode=cc0_only; max 1 CC dispatch at a time. Budget gate in Granny (usage check) provides a second cap.
  - Blast radius: if role_map empty/missing, Granny falls back to worker= field inference — existing behavior, no runaway. Worst case is stalled queue, not runaway spend.

**Affected files:** None — observation and verification only
**Design rules:** none apply
**Scope boundary:** Observe predicted effects; annotate outcome as occurred / clear / partial and close
**Completion criteria:** no automated verification — observation ticket; done when predicted effects are annotated and ticket is closed
